---
title: "heatmap S1 ENDseq hDNA
output: 
date: "2026-02-20"
---

######################################################################################################
# Gustave Roussy (GR) | CNRS UMR 9019, Genome dynamics and instability Lab - Gabriel MATOS RODRIGUES 
# Developed by BEKKOUCHE Louisa - bekkouche.louisa@gmail.com                                 
# Edited by: Genome dynamics and instability Lab                                     
# v.0.1 - 2025/12/18 (for Human genome) | v.0.2 2026/02/16 (for Mouse genenome)	     
# R version 4.5.2 (2025-10-31)                                                       
# MIT licence - Copyright (c) 2025 Gabriel Matos Rodrigues			                     
# run it in a env conda for R version and enjoy your results! :)                     
#######################################################################################################

```{r}

#######################################################################################################

# Heatmap generation of BigWig signals around genomic regions
# - Defines input directories and output folder
# - Loads a BED file and two BigWig files (control & case)
# - Converts regions into a GRanges object
# - Extracts signal matrices using normalizeToMatrix()
# - Orders rows based on signal intensity
# - Builds two aligned EnrichedHeatmap objects (ctrl and case)
#  -Exports the final figure in PDF and PNG formats
# - Requirements:
#   - EnrichedHeatmap
#   - ComplexHeatmap
#   - circlize
#   - GenomicRanges / IRanges
#   - rtracklayer

#######################################################################################################

# Packages installation CRAN + BIOCONDUCTOR

# Install BiocManager
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages("BiocManager", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
}

cran_pkgs <- c("circlize", "grid")
bioc_pkgs <- c("GenomicRanges","IRanges","rtracklayer","EnrichedHeatmap","ComplexHeatmap")

# Utility function: installs only missing packages
install_missing <- function(pkgs) {
    missing <- pkgs[!pkgs %in% installed.packages()[, "Package"]]
    if (length(missing) > 0)
        install.packages(missing, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
}

# Install missing CRAN packages
install_missing(cran_pkgs)

# Install missing Bioconductor packages
BiocManager::install(bioc_pkgs, ask = FALSE, update = TRUE)

message("All required packages are installed.")

library(GenomicRanges)
library(IRanges)
library(rtracklayer)
library(EnrichedHeatmap)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
library(grid)

########################################################################################################

# User inputs
# Base directory containing BED + BigWig files
base_dir <- "put the file path"

# Cell line or dataset name (used in output filenames)
line <- "put the cell line name"

# Output directory
out_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "put the output folder name")
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Function: generate heatmaps for one cell line / dataset
make_heatmap <- function(line, base_dir, out_dir) {

  message("Processing ", line)

  # Define file paths
  bed_file <- file.path(base_dir, "put the bed file name")

  bw_ctrl <- file.path(base_dir, "put the bw ctrl name")   # Control BigWig
  bw_case <- file.path(base_dir, "put the bw case name")   # Case / treated BigWig

  # Load BED file and convert to GRanges
  # BED must contain: chr, start, end (first 3 columns)
  regions <- read.table(bed_file, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)[, 1:3]
  colnames(regions) <- c("chr", "start", "end")

  # Convert to GRanges for genomic operations
  regions_gr <- GRanges(
    seqnames = regions$chr,
    ranges   = IRanges(start = regions$start, end = regions$end)
  )

  # Extract signal matrices from BigWig files
  bw_files <- list(ctrl = bw_ctrl, case = bw_case)

  signal_matrices <- lapply(bw_files, function(bw) {
    bw_data <- import(bw)  # Import BigWig signal

    # Convert signal track into a matrix aligned around regions
    normalizeToMatrix(
      bw_data,
      regions_gr,
      extend        = 500,       # number of bp upstream/downstream
      value_column  = "score",
      mean_mode     = "coverage",
      w             = 25,        # bin size
      smooth        = FALSE,
      background    = 0
    )
  })


  # Convert to numeric matrices
  mat1 <- as.matrix(signal_matrices[[1]])  # Control
  mat2 <- as.matrix(signal_matrices[[2]])  # Case

  # Color scale for heatmaps
  col <- colorRamp2(
    c(0, 1, 2),                  # signal values
    c("navy", "purple", "orange")  # corresponding colors
  )


  # Order rows by signal intensity
  row_order_1 <- order(rowSums(mat1, na.rm = TRUE), decreasing = TRUE)
  row_order_2 <- order(rowSums(mat2, na.rm = TRUE), decreasing = TRUE)

  mat_1_ord <- mat1[row_order_1, , drop = FALSE]
  mat_2_ord <- mat2[row_order_2, , drop = FALSE]

  # Shared legend parameters
  legend_param <- list(
    title  = "",
    at     = c(0, 0.75, 1.5),
    labels = c("Low", "", "High")
  )

  # Build heatmap: CONTROL
  ht <-
    EnrichedHeatmap(
      mat_1_ord,
      name = "ctrl",
      col  = col,
      heatmap_legend_param = legend_param,
      pos_line = FALSE,
      axis_name = c("-500", "", "", "500"),
      width  = unit(3.2, "cm"),
      height = unit(8.1, "cm"),
      top_annotation = HeatmapAnnotation(
        enriched = anno_enriched(
          gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 2),
          ylim = c(0, 1.5),
          axis_param = list(
            at     = c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5),
            labels = character(0),
            side   = "right",
            facing = "outside"
          )
        )
      )
    ) +

    # Heatmap: CASE
    EnrichedHeatmap(
      mat_2_ord,
      name = "case",
      col  = col,
      heatmap_legend_param = list(
        title = NULL,
        at     = NULL,
        labels = NULL
      ),
      show_heatmap_legend = FALSE,
      pos_line = FALSE,
      axis_name = c("-500", "", "", "500"),
      width  = unit(3.2, "cm"),
      height = unit(8.1, "cm"),
      top_annotation = HeatmapAnnotation(
        enriched = anno_enriched(
          gp = gpar(col = "blue", lwd = 2),
          ylim = c(0, 1.5),
          axis_param = list(
            at     = c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5),
            labels = c("0", "0.5", "1", "1.5"),
            side   = "right",
            facing = "outside"
          )
        )
      )
    )

  # Export figures (PDF + PNG)
  pdf(
    file.path(out_dir, paste0(line, "_heatmap.pdf")),
    width  = 7,
    height = 6
  )
  draw(ht, heatmap_legend_side = "right")
  dev.off()

  png(
    file.path(out_dir, paste0(line, "_heatmap.png")),
    width  = 11,
    height = 6,
    units  = "in",
    res    = 300,
    type   = "cairo"
  )
  draw(ht, heatmap_legend_side = "right")
  dev.off()
}


# Run the function on cell line in 'line'
lapply(
  line,
  make_heatmap,
  base_dir = base_dir,
  out_dir  = out_dir
)

##########################################################################################################################

```
